auth.redaction: the token must never appear in exception text or logs

create.body: {name, config: {served_entities: [{name, entity_name, entity_version, workload_size, scale_to_zero_enabled}], traffic_config: {routes: [{served_model_name, traffic_percentage}]}}}
create.success: 200 with the ServingEndpointDetailed document (state.ready NOT_READY, state.config_update IN_PROGRESS, pending_config present while deploying)

get.success: 200 with ServingEndpointDetailed: name, creation_timestamp, last_updated_timestamp, state {ready, config_update}, config and/or pending_config {start_time, config_version, served_entities, traffic_config}, endpoint_url when routable
get.missing: 404 with {error_code: RESOURCE_DOES_NOT_EXIST} when the endpoint has never been created

update_config.body: {served_entities, traffic_config} — the endpoint name lives in the path, NOT in the body
update_config.conflict: while an update is in progress another update cannot be made: 409 with {error_code: RESOURCE_CONFLICT}; the conflicted PUT must not be blindly retried

states.deployment_success: an endpoint is done deploying when state.ready == READY and state.config_update == NOT_UPDATING
states.deployment_terminal_failure: config_update UPDATE_FAILED or UPDATE_CANCELED is terminal — polling must stop immediately
states.served_entity_state: each served entity carries state {deployment, deployment_state_message}; failed entities keep their message and must be preserved per entity

pending_config.note: while an update deploys, the incoming entities (and their per-entity deployment states) live under pending_config, not config

polling.policy: bounded: at most max_polls waits of poll_interval through the injected wait; exceeding the bound is a typed timeout error carrying the last observed states; caller cancellation must abort promptly
